creator: sheldonh@uunet.co.za
text:
On Tue, 24 Aug 1999 08:27:02 -0400, "Crist J. Clark" wrote:

> One minor addition, when refering to, 'the set command,' shouldn't it
> actually be, 'the set(1) command.' That is how it is written when
> mentioned elsewhere in the page.

My feeling is that _none_ of the builtins should have section indicators
associated with them. Try ``man set''. :-)

So here's what I propose:

	* Fix grammar and spelling nits.
	* Use .Dq and .Qq where appropriate.
	* Divorce trailing punctuation from quoted elements of .Dq .
	* Use .Dq instead of .Xr for builtins.
	* Remove trailing whitespace and blank lines.

Ciao,
Sheldon.

 .Ss Shell Patterns
 A pattern consists of normal characters, which match themselves,
 and meta-characters.   The meta-characters are
-``!'', ``*'', ``?'', and ``[''.  These characters lose

-or variable substitution is performed and the dollar sign

+.Dq ! ,

+These characters lose their special meanings if they are quoted.
+When command or variable substitution is performed and the dollar sign

 variable or the output of the command is scanned for these
 characters and they are turned into meta-characters.

 set or displayed.  While everybody is allowed to reduce a

 The remaining options specify which resource value is to be
